mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and push the 10-year Treasury yield upward as market participants price in prolonged rate persistence.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% near threshold, CPIAUCSL at 320.580
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of instances when trigger condition is met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This triggers flight-to-safety demand for Treasuries, driving the 10-year yield downward as investors reduce equity exposure and increase duration holdings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2%, near historical lows
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of instances when unemployment increases by > 0.4pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure aggregate demand strength. When both decline simultaneously by > 2% month-over-month, it signals weakening economic momentum. This contraction expectation causes the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as growth forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND HOUST declines > 2% MoM
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of instances when both demand proxies decline simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 104.0 (index 2017=100) indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization. Sustained high production signals inflation persistence and justifies higher terminal rates, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward as the Fed maintains restrictive policy longer.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.8799, just below threshold
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of instances when INDPRO remains elevated, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month, signaling labor market cooling. This slowdown reduces wage pressure and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as the market reprices the terminal rate lower.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100K for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of instances when payroll growth falls below 100K, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized, indicating robust economic expansion. This growth surprise raises inflation expectations and terminal rate forecasts, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward as the market reprices long-duration risk premium.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of quarters with GDP growth > 3%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS declines below 4.0% while CPIAUCSL remains above 318, the Fed enters a cutting cycle amid persistent inflation. This policy pivot signals economic weakness and rate-cut expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as investors extend duration and reduce equity risk.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of instances when Fed cuts rates amid elevated inflation, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 103.0 while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, signaling stagflationary pressure with weakening supply and labor slack. This regime shift causes the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as recession fears dominate inflation concerns and the Fed faces policy constraints.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of instances when both INDPRO and UNRATE meet stagflationary thresholds, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.
